mei.relationship: Both companies have equivalent capabilities. No clear value differential in the outsourcing relationship
mei.daily_feel: Collaboration is smooth but unremarkable. Like doing business with another version of yourself
mei.energy_flow_desc: Balanced and equal. No extraction, but no complementary value-add either
mei.long_term_risk: Once the client fills their internal capacity gap, they no longer need you. Contract can end anytime
mei.growth: Low. You're doing what you already know. No new learning
mei.stability: Medium. Stable during the contract term, but weak renewal motivation
mei.bad_year_impact: In a bad year, if the client cuts outsourcing budget, you're the first to go
mei.fit_for: Short-term capacity augmentation. Not suitable as a primary client

hr.relationship: Vendor capabilities overlap with internal team. Outsourcing value is mainly headcount, not expertise
hr.daily_feel: Collaboration is smooth but the vendor adds limited incremental value
hr.bad_year_impact: Outsourcing contracts are the first item cut during budget reductions
hr.fit_for: Pure capacity supplementation needs. Not suited for long-term strategic partnership

gyo.relationship: Your company unconsciously over-invests. A sense of mission drives you to make the client's project excellent
gyo.daily_feel: You voluntarily work overtime to raise delivery quality. You aim for the best even when the client hasn't asked for it
gyo.energy_flow_desc: You invest slightly more than required, but the client's responsiveness keeps it from becoming draining
gyo.long_term_risk: The Gyo-side company tends to prioritize client needs over its own team's needs
gyo.growth: Medium. Experience accumulates through serving the client, but direction is client-determined
gyo.stability: Medium-high. Both sides have a rapport foundation, and contract renewal rates are high
gyo.bad_year_impact: In a bad year, the input-to-output ratio worsens, but the relationship holds
gyo.fit_for: Teams willing to invest deeply in client relationships and treat client success as their own

hr.relationship: Vendor brings intrinsic motivation and invests beyond contract requirements
hr.daily_feel: Vendor delivery quality is consistently above average with good responsiveness
hr.bad_year_impact: In a bad year, input-to-output ratio worsens but the collaboration remains stable
hr.fit_for: Partners who demonstrate high responsiveness and willingness to go beyond contracted scope

tai.relationship: The client naturally provides resources and guidance. Your company grows within their ecosystem
tai.daily_feel: Like being mentored by a larger organization. The client proactively shares specs and technical resources
tai.energy_flow_desc: The client invests guidance resources; you're the beneficiary. Low drain
tai.long_term_risk: Over-dependence on a single client. After leaving, you may not know how to win work independently
tai.growth: Medium. You grow at the client's pace but are constrained by their tech stack
tai.stability: Medium-high. The client has a vendor development strategy and won't switch easily
tai.bad_year_impact: In a bad year, the client's ecosystem provides a buffer. Lower risk than independent contracting
tai.fit_for: Small-to-mid-size outsourcing teams that want to leverage enterprise resources for growth

hr.relationship: Vendor grows within the client's ecosystem; client invests guidance resources
hr.daily_feel: Vendor adapts quickly with good return on training investment
hr.bad_year_impact: In a bad year, ecosystem buffer prevents sudden contract termination
hr.fit_for: Vendors willing to follow the client's tech stack for long-term development

ei.relationship: Your delivery quality directly boosts client revenue, and client feedback continuously improves your team
ei.daily_feel: A positive feedback loop where both sides elevate each other. The more projects you do together, the better the coordination
ei.energy_flow_desc: You invest high-quality deliverables; client feedback prevents any feeling of drain
ei.long_term_risk: The Ei side invests more. If the client suddenly cuts budget, the positive loop breaks
ei.growth: High. Bidirectional improvement where your team grows continuously through the collaboration
ei.stability: Medium-high. Stable as long as the positive loop continues. Risk factor: client-side procurement personnel changes
ei.bad_year_impact: In a bad year, collaboration pace may slow but won't flip into a drain relationship
ei.fit_for: Teams willing to invest in high-quality delivery and believe in long-term collaboration value

hr.relationship: Vendor and client drive each other's business growth in a positive feedback loop
hr.daily_feel: Collaboration efficiency improves continuously with both delivery quality and feedback on track
hr.bad_year_impact: In a bad year, pace slows but doesn't reverse. Collaboration foundation is solid
hr.fit_for: Vendors capable of sustained high-quality delivery. Ideal for long-term strategic partnership

shin.relationship: The client invests resources to develop your team. You are the beneficiary
shin.daily_feel: The client provides specs, training, and technical documentation. Entry barriers are lowered
shin.energy_flow_desc: The client actively invests; you are the beneficiary. Low drain, fast growth
shin.long_term_risk: After leaving this client, others may not provide the same level of resources
shin.growth: High. Passive but effective growth. The key is internalizing what you learn as your own capability
shin.stability: High. The client has a vendor development strategy and won't switch easily
shin.bad_year_impact: In a bad year, client investment may shrink but won't reverse into drain
shin.fit_for: Teams with strong learning ability that can quickly internalize client knowledge

hr.relationship: Client invests resources to develop the vendor. Vendor is the beneficiary
hr.daily_feel: Vendor ramps up quickly with good return on training investment
hr.bad_year_impact: In a bad year, development intensity weakens but no reverse drain
hr.fit_for: Outsourcing teams with strong learning ability that can reach delivery standards quickly

yu.relationship: Your company is the giver. The client enjoys your service but reciprocation is limited
yu.daily_feel: You proactively serve, communicate, and solve problems. The client considers this standard
yu.energy_flow_desc: You continuously over-deliver; the client receives. Your team's fatigue goes unnoticed
yu.long_term_risk: The Yu-side company sees margin compression. Team burnout risk is high
yu.growth: Medium. Client relationship management skills improve, but profit margins shrink
yu.stability: Medium. Relationship stays stable as long as you keep over-delivering; stop and you may lose the client
yu.bad_year_impact: In a bad year, your delivery capacity drops and previously 'free' services become unsustainable
yu.fit_for: Teams with strong business development capability that can leverage this client to acquire others

hr.relationship: Vendor continuously over-delivers; client enjoys above-contract service
hr.daily_feel: Vendor service quality is high but actual costs may exceed contract value
hr.bad_year_impact: In a bad year, vendor delivery capacity drops and service quality may plummet
hr.fit_for: Projects requiring high service quality, but watch for vendor burnout risk

sui.relationship: The client provides projects and resources, but your team gets increasingly drained
sui.daily_feel: The client's projects look good on paper, but actual execution burden is heavier than expected
sui.energy_flow_desc: The client drives the collaboration pace; your team follows reactively. Year one is manageable; after that, diminishing returns
sui.long_term_risk: By the time you want to exit, dependencies are too deep to disengage
sui.growth: High but passive. The client pushes your team to grow, but the direction is client-determined
sui.stability: Medium-low. Team fatigue accumulates; key personnel may resign
sui.bad_year_impact: A bad year combined with Sui-side drain may be more than the team can handle
sui.fit_for: Teams with clear contract end dates and a defined exit timeline

hr.relationship: Client provides resources but vendor team is continuously drained
hr.daily_feel: Vendor performs well initially but output diminishes over time
hr.bad_year_impact: In a bad year, vendor may be unable to maintain delivery quality
hr.fit_for: Short-to-medium-term collaboration with explicit exit mechanisms. Otherwise, retention risk is high

an.relationship: Your company handles project stability but needs to continuously output management energy
an.daily_feel: Like holding an umbrella over the client's entire project. PM and tech support burden falls on you
an.energy_flow_desc: You continuously output management and stabilization energy; the client depends on your steadiness
an.long_term_risk: The An-side company's management resources get locked to this client, unavailable for other engagements
an.growth: Medium. Project management skills improve, but you're stuck in the stabilizer role
an.stability: Appears high, actually draining. Stable while you hold the umbrella; collapse when you can't
an.bad_year_impact: In a bad year, stabilization pressure doubles and can become the straw that breaks the team
an.fit_for: Teams with strong management skills that can set boundaries. Without boundaries, you'll be emptied out

hr.relationship: Vendor handles project stability but management energy drain is significant
hr.daily_feel: Vendor holds the project together, but sustained umbrella-holding carries burnout risk
hr.bad_year_impact: In a bad year, stabilization pressure doubles and vendor may demand contract renegotiation
hr.fit_for: Management-strong outsourcing teams. Pay attention to management responsibility allocation in the contract

kai.relationship: The client continuously extracts value from your company. Endless revisions, payment delays, scope explosions
kai.daily_feel: Initially you think the client is just demanding. Six months later, you realize nothing is ever enough
kai.energy_flow_desc: The client unilaterally drains your delivery energy. Margins fall continuously
kai.long_term_risk: Halfway through the project you're already in the red, but the penalty for walking away costs more
kai.growth: Low. All team energy goes to handling client revisions and scope additions
kai.stability: Low. The Kai side is the most unstable position. Highest risk of contract disputes
kai.bad_year_impact: A bad year combined with Kai position may mean contract disputes or financial crisis
kai.fit_for: Unless contract terms strictly protect you, this client is not recommended

hr.relationship: Client continuously exploits vendor. Payment delays and scope explosions are the norm
hr.daily_feel: Vendor starts flagging after six months with declining delivery quality and attitude
hr.bad_year_impact: In a bad year, vendor may breach contract and walk away
hr.fit_for: Without strict scope and payment protection clauses, this collaboration carries extreme risk

ki.relationship: The client's pressure pushes your team to grow. Pressure-driven collaboration
ki.daily_feel: High client demands and strict standards. Short-term pressure, but it forges good work
ki.energy_flow_desc: The client applies pressure; your team absorbs it. Drain is non-trivial but offset by capability growth
ki.long_term_risk: The Ki side has a pressure tolerance ceiling. Beyond it, growth turns to damage
ki.growth: High but costly. Pressure-driven growth requires team rest mechanisms
ki.stability: Medium. Manageable pressure is good training; excessive pressure triggers resignations
ki.bad_year_impact: In a bad year, pressure may crush the team rather than push it forward
ki.fit_for: Companies with high team stress tolerance that thrive on demanding clients

hr.relationship: Client's high standards drive vendor growth. Pressure-type collaboration
hr.daily_feel: Vendor shows drive in the short term, but long-term burnout needs monitoring
hr.bad_year_impact: In a bad year, pressure may crush the vendor. Delivery pace adjustment needed
hr.fit_for: High-stress-tolerance outsourcing companies with stable teams

sei.relationship: Your team becomes stronger through alignment with the client's high standards. Delivery quality improves continuously
sei.daily_feel: Initially uncomfortable during standards alignment. After adapting, you realize the team has leveled up
sei.energy_flow_desc: The client's requirements forge your team. Low drain but solid growth
sei.long_term_risk: Sei-side risk is low, but the initial alignment period may cause impatient team members to leave
sei.growth: High. Growth through client alignment is solid and transferable to other engagements
sei.stability: High. Post-alignment, this is one of the most stable collaboration types
sei.bad_year_impact: In a bad year, the alignment friction intensifies but doesn't break the collaboration
sei.fit_for: Companies patient enough to endure the alignment period and focused on long-term team growth

hr.relationship: Vendor grows through alignment with client's high standards. Delivery quality improves continuously
hr.daily_feel: Vendor needs initial adaptation but becomes stable and continuously improving post-alignment
hr.bad_year_impact: In a bad year, alignment friction increases but the collaboration holds
hr.fit_for: Outsourcing teams willing to endure alignment and invest for the long term
